text:
Insurance (, Hoken) is the 300 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Ikalgo is shown leaving the basement, and he's shocked to see that so much of the palace has been destroyed in only half an hour. One of the fallen towers begins crumbling, forcing him to escape the rubble. Someone suddenly calls his name, and he spots Killua accompanied by Palm. The three sit and talk, with Ikalgo thrilled that Palm is alive, saying that he can drive her to Peijin as they planned. But Killua tells him that she wants to stay, and adds that she's probably stronger now than he is. Palm longs to see Gon, but says she wants to respect his wishes, confusing Ikalgo. Killua clarifies that Gon plans to fight Pitou on his own, and Ikalgo realizes that they're staying behind because Gon has no chance against the Royal Guard, and neither do they, so they plan to stay and die together.

Killua mentions that they're looking for Knuckle, and Ikalgo agrees to help. Some time passes, and the three meet up with Knuckle and Meleoron, who tell them that they separated the Royal Guards and Netero has taken out the King. Since they've done what they were supposed to, Knuckle asks them if they should leave or stay, personally saying that they should fight until the very end. The rest silently agree, and they all begin walking toward the palace.

Gon rises to his feet and tells Pitou their time is up, leaving them in shock at how he knew that the surgery was finished when they had tried to hide it so well. As Pitou continues to worry about Gon's heightened awareness, he tells them to stand up and that they will make it to Peijin more quickly if they run. With Doctor Blythe deactivated, Pitou anxiously rises as Komugi awakens and asks what happened to her. Pitou begins to say something to Gon, but his expression suddenly darkens as he threatens to kill Komugi if Pitou talks out of turn again. With Komugi scared, Pitou agrees to follow him, and it's stated that Pitou could have taken off Gon's head at that moment, but restrained themselves, understanding how sharp his perception was. They know their chance will come, and that they have to somehow transfer Komugi to the King during their journey to Peijin.

But Killua and Knuckle suddenly appear, with the latter asking Gon if he wants him to use Hakoware to be able to keep track of Pitou if they escape (with A.P.R. also acting as a shield for him if they attack). Gon calmly refuses the offer, saying that he trusts Pitou, and tells him to take care of Komugi until he and Pitou reach Kite. Both Knuckle and Pitou immediately realize that he's using her as a hostage, and Gon promises to release her once Kite is healed. He then sternly asks if Pitou will trust him as well, sweat dripping down their face, and the two sprint off toward Peijin.

The rest of the group stands around Komugi, and Ikalgo asks Killua if he's sure he didn't want to go with them. Killua says it's fine, and that he was glad when he heard the hostage plan since if Pitou were to capture him, he would become one himself and a hostage exchange could ruin things. Knuckle shudders at Killua's cold composure, wondering how much he's been through to think this way. Palm assures them that she can see what happens since she saw Gon with her eye, and Killua reminds them that Pitou won't abandon Komugi, who trembles with fear and confusion at the whole ordeal.
